claim: The workflow creates a complete three-phase N-1 dataset from scenario and contingency catalogs.
The binary violation label is consistent with voltage, loading, VUF, service-loss, and non-convergence flags.
evidence: week8_violation_summary.csv; label consistency proof in Notebook 08

claim: The best zero-missed-violation holdout model is GradientBoosting with PF calls saved fraction 0.400.
Group-based CV provides a stricter test than random holdout because scenarios and contingencies are separated by group.
Top-k screening provides an operator-oriented way to reduce the contingency list sent to three-phase power flow.
The current benchmark is a small teaching feeder, so results should not be claimed as broad deployment evidence.
evidence: dataset statistics and Week 7 discussion; future work section
